diffCoverage.notes: Account state is frontend-managed in the ui session store; RailNav (unchanged this slice) renders it. No Rust command added — accounts come from existing login/restore profiles, so the ui->core boundary is unchanged.

findings.evidence: switchAccount/activate operate entirely on the ui session store's per-account buckets (Map<accountId, AccountData>); the reactive view fields are swapped to the target bucket. No new Tauri command and no core change — the account set is derived from the AccountProfiles returned by login/qr/restore. The added switch diagnostic logs account ids + a conversation count only.
findings.fix: None required. If active-account persistence across restarts is wanted later, store the selection in the local store.

summary: Architecture review: the account switcher is correctly frontend-managed — buckets keyed by accountId in the ui session store, RailNav rendering, no backend command needed (accounts come from login/restore profiles). The ui->core boundary is unchanged. Approve.
